Title: Oral Gavage of Ginger Nanoparticle-Derived Lipid Vectors Carrying Dmt1 siRNA Blunts Iron Loading in Murine Hereditary Hemochromatosis

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: 129S6 background mice; mouse cDNA also used in inducible HEK293 assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential micronutrient for erythropoiesis and systemic oxygen transport; excess causes tissue iron overload in hereditary hemochromatosis
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plasma; liver; heart; kidney; pancreas; spleen
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: intestine-specific knockout; double knockout with Hepc-/-; Ussing chamber 59Fe flux assay; qRT-PCR; oral siRNA knockdown via FA-GDLVs; tissue non-heme iron profiling; serum ferritin and TSAT measurements
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes intestinal iron absorption and is required for pathological iron loading in Hepc-/- mice; intestinal silencing or ablation blunts tissue iron accumulation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in duodenum/proximal small intestine; induced during iron deficiency/anemia
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in duodenal epithelium and proximal jejunum; specifically downregulated in duodenal epithelium by FA-GDLV-siRNA treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Intestinal ablation in Hepc-/- mice causes severe anemia, reduced body and liver weights, enlarged spleen and heart, increased erythropoietic demand; therapeutic knockdown was partial to mitigate overload while avoiding major hematologic toxicity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Validated therapeutic target for oral RNAi to reduce intestinal iron uptake in hereditary hemochromatosis and potentially other iron-loading disorders; partial intestinal-specific suppression may be preferable to full ablation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Iron-deficiency anemia/phlebotomy; oral FA-GDLV-delivered siRNA; standard chow followed by low-iron diet; pH 6.5 transport conditions in Ussing chamber
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core gene of the study. Dmt1 was shown to be the predominant active intestinal iron importer during anemia, and intestinal Dmt1 was required for iron overload in juvenile HH model. siRNA knockdown reduced duodenal Dmt1 mRNA by ~50% and lowered systemic/tissue iron without detectable inflammation or off-target knockdown in liver, kidney, or bone marrow
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ussing chamber analyses showed near-zero duodenal 59Fe flux in Dmt1int/int mice, demonstrating Dmt1 is the principal intestinal iron importer during anemia. 2. In Hepc-/- mice, intestine-specific loss of Dmt1 abolished iron loading in plasma and tissues. 3. Oral FA-GDLV delivery of Dmt1 siRNA specifically reduced duodenal Dmt1 expression and significantly decreased serum ferritin, TSAT, and tissue non-heme iron.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Systemic iron is essential for erythropoiesis, but excess causes iron overload pathology
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Plasma; liver; heart; kidney; pancreas; spleen
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Peptide hormone regulator of iron homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Systemic negative regulation of iron export/absorption pathway
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Iron homeostasis, intestinal absorption, systemic distribution/export
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene knockout; double-mutant genetic interaction with intestine-specific Dmt1 knockout; tissue non-heme iron profiling; hematology and iron indices
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of Hepc causes iron overload, but this phenotype depends on intestinal Dmt1; in double mutants iron loading is abolished and severe anemia develops
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Fpn1-mediated iron export pathway; indirectly determines enterocyte iron status and Dmt1 expression context
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Loss of Hepc permits high Fpn1 activity and creates conditions favoring elevated intestinal iron uptake via Dmt1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential systemic suppressor of iron loading; its absence creates hereditary hemochromatosis phenotype used in this study
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Hepc loss causes severe systemic iron overload; combined with intestinal Dmt1 ablation causes severe anemia and increased erythropoietic demand
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports therapeutic rationale for targeting intestinal Dmt1 downstream of hepcidin deficiency in hereditary hemochromatosis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Low-iron diet used during therapeutic intervention; juvenile stage of disease progression
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core regulatory gene because Hepc knockout is experimentally central to establishing Dmt1-dependent iron loading in the Results section
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hepc knockout mice accumulate iron in multiple tissues beginning at 2–3 weeks of age. 2. Removing intestinal Dmt1 from Hepc-/- mice abolishes iron loading. 3. The double-mutant phenotype demonstrates that pathological iron loading caused by Hepc deficiency requires intestinal Dmt1.
